  • Recipes

  • Turkey
  • Ground Turkey Recipes

Spinach and Feta Turkey Burgers

4.6
(860)
645 Reviews
77 Photos

These spinach and feta turkey burgers are a big hit in the summertime when everyone is tired of the same old hamburgers and hot dogs. Serve with or without buns and your favorite condiments. I like to serve these with lettuce, tomato, and tzatziki sauce.

Submitted by FoodieGeek
Updated on December 18, 2025
Tested by
Allrecipes Test Kitchen
Tested by Allrecipes Test Kitchen
The Allrecipes Test Kitchen staff are a team of culinary pros who make and vet recipes using only equipment you'd find in a home kitchen. Staff credentials vary but include training at the Culinary Institute of America; kitchen stints in fine dining restaurants; and countless hours in the test kitchen itself.
Meet the Allrecipes Test Kitchen
77
77
77
77
Prep Time:
20 mins
Cook Time:
15 mins
Total Time:
35 mins
Servings:
8
Yield:
8 turkey burgers

Why You'll Love This Recipe

  • These grilled turkey burgers are blended with spinach and feta for a flavorful twist.
  • Home cooks praise the juiciness of the patties and often top them with tzatziki sauce.
  • "I used feta mixed with sun dried tomatoes and basil," shares Allrecipes member Shannon Klein.
Keep Screen Awake

Ingredients

Oops! Something went wrong. Our team is working on it.

This recipe was developed at its original yield. Ingredient amounts are automatically adjusted, but cooking times and steps remain unchanged. Note that not all recipes scale perfectly. Original recipe (1X) yields 8 servings

  • cooking spray

  • 2 pounds ground turkey

  • 1 (10 ounce) box frozen chopped spinach, thawed and squeezed dry

  • 4 ounces feta cheese

  • 2 large eggs, beaten

  • 2 cloves garlic, minced

Directions

  1. Preheat an outdoor grill for medium-high heat and lightly oil the grate.

  2. Mix together turkey, spinach, feta, eggs, and garlic in a large bowl until well combined; form into 8 patties.

  3. Cook patties on the preheated grill on both sides until no longer pink in the center, 15 to 20 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center of patties should read at least 165 degrees F (74 degrees C).

Editor's Note

You can find recipes for Tzatziki Sauce to top your burgers.

1,776 home cooks made it!
Something went wrong. Please reload and try again.

Nutrition Facts (per serving)

233 Calories
13g Fat
2g Carbs
27g Protein

Servings Per Recipe 8
Calories 233
% Daily Value *

Dietary Fiber 1g 4%
Total Sugars 1g
Protein 27g 55%
Total Fat 13g 17%
Saturated Fat 5g 24%
Cholesterol 143mg 48%
Vitamin C 2mg 2%
Sodium 266mg 12%
Calcium 144mg 11%
Iron 3mg 14%
Potassium 373mg 8%

* Percent Daily Values are based on a 2,000 calorie diet. Your daily values may be higher or lower depending on your calorie needs.

** Nutrient information is not available for all ingredients. Amount is based on available nutrient data.

Photos of Spinach and Feta Turkey Burgers

01 of 77
Photo by  Happyschmoopies
02 of 77
Photo by  cwrutledge
04/26/2023
Sooo good!!! I served mine on flat bread topped with carmelized red onion, bean sprouts, tomato, cucumber and homemade tzatziki. My kids were wary at first but they have all asked me to make it again next week!!
03 of 77
Photo by  Nick Walls
05/24/2021
04 of 77
Photo by  Andy
08/02/2020
05 of 77
Photo by  Debbie
07/02/2020
06 of 77
Photo by  Pat J
06/30/2019
07 of 77
Photo by  Kim Raposo
05/26/2019
Great recipe! I am not a fan of ground turkey but love this!
08 of 77
Photo by  IrishMorgans
05/22/2019
These burgers were epic! So good and so healthy. Thanks for the share. Just added more garlic. Amazing. Will be making again and again - less red meat is a win in our house.
09 of 77
Photo by  Froggie
05/20/2019
I added feta and frozen kale. Ah-ma-zing!!! Cooked the burgers on indoor grill doing quarter turns every three minutes four times (12minutes). Garnished with arugula and tomato slice.
10 of 77
Photo by  Devin Magee
05/18/2019
this was an outstanding recipe. I only added poblano peppers to give it a little kick
